title: [common] extract thumbnail/s from JSON-LD
body:
### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [x] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
- [x] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
- [x] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [x] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [x] Improvement

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

The Json-ld extractor assumes `thumbnailUrl` is a string but it can be a list.
This fix sets `thumbnail` if a `compat_str` is found and `thumbnails` if a `list` is found.



Real-world example in this page: https://www.rainews.it/video/2021/12/Tg3-Frida-Bollani-8f449635-e334-4105-8357-563f2bde1158.html I could'n understand why it did't extracted the thumbnail and so I made this fix to the common.py file

title: [youtube] Fix inconsistent date timezones (ensure result is in UTC)
body:
## Please follow the guide below

- You will be asked some questions, please read them **carefully** and answer honestly
- Put an `x` into all the boxes [ ] relevant to your *pull request* (like that [x])

- [X] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
- [X] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [X] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [X] Bug fix
- [ ] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

Fixes #1881 
Depends on #2069 

- Fixes upload_date for videos not being in UTC timezone (by parsing the `dateText` instead)
    - player response upload date is fixed to a timezone such as PT (US timezone). We will only use this as a fallback now.
    - this inadvertently fixes upload dates not being available on non-web clients (#1067)
    - For videos where there may be relative time, this would be restricted to <24 hours, so will still be accurate.
    - Livestreams, premieres and scheduled videos will continue to use microformat upload date, since in those cases the upload date is the true upload date. This changes to published date once the video is published.
~~- Fixes `datetime_from_str` using computer's timezone rather than UTC~~ already merged
   ~~- relative time text parsing actually returns a UNIX timestamp in UTC~~
~~- Enforce UTC timezone for all API/webpage calls in YouTube extractors, regardless of cookie setting.~~ already merged

TODO:
 - [X] Add some tests

title: Extend file access retries to check rename and remove as well
body:
## Please follow the guide below

- You will be asked some questions, please read them **carefully** and answer honestly
- Put an `x` into all the boxes [ ] relevant to your *pull request* (like that [x])
- Use *Preview* tab to see how your *pull request* will actually look like

---

### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [x] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
- [x] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
- [x] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [x] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

I'm still getting some file access errors on my laptop, so I extended the logic to cover a few more places. I added `try_remove`, matching `try_rename` to avoid bare `os.remove` calls. To avoid code duplication I turned the retry logic into a decorator that wraps `sanitize_open`, `try_remove` and `try_rename`.

Notes:
- `try_rename` had a custom error message. I couldn't add it to the decorator and I think it would have conflicted with the error handling so it now raises the built in error if it fails.
- I added `error.EINVAL` (error 22) to the list of errors to retry, as I also saw this error on my laptop (I'm pretty sure it's the same problem as the parameter looks fine and it doesn't fail every time).

* Crashes when there are no subtitles
* No tests

New extractor for the **unprotected** content from Netflix (just the trailers and the season recaps that unregistered users can see on the show/movie landing page). The extractor supports VP9, AV1[^1], H265, H264 video up to 4k (for AV1 and H265) and can pull audio tracks in all available languages.

[^1]: The profile I got from the web player mentioned it but I haven't found a suitable content actually using it. Maybe it needs more tweaking or it's just for selected content for the subscribers.

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

This is an update of [youtube-dl PR 22661](https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/pull/22661). It makes the following changes to the veoh extractor:

- Accept `videos` URLs.
- Prefer high quality (current master incorrectly marks "Regular" rather than "HQ" as "best").
- Extract metadata for `age_limit`, `categories`, and `tags`.

It also updates the test cases and removes the defunct `_extract_video` method.

Thank you!

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

In the YouTube extractor, this removes the v2 onions as support for them has finally been [removed](https://support.torproject.org/onionservices/v2-deprecation/) from Tor, and adds a couple Invidious instances as found in the [documentation](https://docs.invidious.io/Invidious-Instances.md), it does not remove the several that seem down though.

In the PornHub extractor, it replaces the v2 onion address with the current v3 address.

I'd like to sort the Invidious onion instances, but figured that'd make too much noise.

- [ ] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

* Simplify regex to avoid catastrophic backtracking, noticed after #2129
  * Loosely match anything between the tag and the AV-pair of interest (authored by @pukkandan)
  * Only match up to the AV-pair of interest, rather than the end of the end of the opening tag
  * Strictly match an unquoted value
  * Likewise determine if quotes are optional around the matched value
  * Avoid `re.DOTALL` as `.` is not used
* Yield directly in `re.finditer`, rather than append to and return a list
  * Noop as both calling functions perform list comprehension
  * But adapt direct calls in `test_utils.test_get_elements_text_and_html_by_attribute`

This is an extractor for "WatchESPN" videos, i.e. anything on [this page](https://www.espn.com/watch/schedule/_/type/replay/startDate/20220108) not listed as ESPN+. All applicable videos require TV provider and are probably geolocked to United States. As a consequence, the base ESPN extractor has been updated to not match WatchESPN URLs and its WatchESPN tests have been removed.

This pull request should be fully functional as-is but it could use the following improvements:

- Addition of a test to check that ESPN+ videos raise the appropriate error. I tested this on my own but I am not sure how to codify this as a test within yt-dlp.
- Additional metadata to return (e.g. description, dates, etc.). Accessing such information requires calls to a different API pointer which I didn't bother to code in.
- It would be useful if one of the more veteran yt-dlp devs could check that I am following appropriate style/abstraction guidelines.

There is no specific issue raised for this (except for possibly [this one on youtube-dl](https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/issues/25305)) but I feel adding this extractor would be immensely useful considering that:

- ESPN is a very popular platform with replays available of many major sporting events and other TV shows, for those with access to ESPN through their cable TV provider.
- Before about August, such videos could only be downloaded by opening up the video and snooping for the playlist m3u8.
- Since August, ESPN has updated their platform so that only dash DRM videos play. Downloading an unprotected video required making modifications to the snooped URL, which had to be done quickly as the authorization expires quickly. Now, with this extractor, unprotected HLS streams are returned by default, saving many steps for anyone wishing to download these videos.

This PR fixes the Zattoo information extractor (as described in #1244). The most important change is the ability to log in again. Furthermore, it successfully extracts videos from the 7-day-replay, ondemand and recording section.
You need a Zattoo premium account to test it. Unfortunately I cannot provide one. If anyone can provide an account to a maintainer to test this PR, let me know. Works:

 * Videos from the guide (7 day replay)
 * Recorded videos
 * Ondemand videos

Doesn't work:

 * Live TV: The dash streams do get extracted successfully by using `_extract_mpd_formats`, but the streams cannot be downloaded. Here is the output that I get:
 ```
[info] srf_zwei: Downloading 1 format(s): dash-5002-A-0+dash-601-A-2
[download] Destination: srf_zwei 2022-01-10 13_26 [srf_zwei].mp4
[https @ 0x556d7b5f8040] HTTP error 404 Not Found
[dash @ 0x556d7b578480] Failed to open fragment of playlist
[https @ 0x556d7bb56b80] HTTP error 404 Not Found
[dash @ 0x556d7b578480] Failed to open fragment of playlist
```
 * Resellers: They are most definitely still broken, but I cannot fix this, since I do not have access to any of them.

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

This patch adds support for video extraction from tvopen.gr and affiliated ethnos.gr, a popular Greek TV content provider and news outlet.

* Add new IEs
  * TVOpenGrBaseIE: Base IE class
  * TVOpenGrWatchIE: Extract videos from TV VOD pages on tvopen.gr and ethnos.gr
  * TVOpenGrEmbedIE: Extract iframe-embeddable tvopen.gr or ethnos.gr videos
* For embeddable and `tvopengr:watch` videos not on tvopen.gr, redirect to the equivalent page at tvopen.gr/watch/... which includes better metadata
  * `VideoObject` JSON-LD
  * full (rather than truncated) description (extracted from a `span[@class='description']` element content)
* TVOpenGrWatchIE queries an API endpoint to get the respective stream/source URLs and then detect formats/subtitles
* The thumbnail endpoint dynamically scales image per specified width, therefore modify the thumbnail URL to use the maximum width among extracted formats

title: [youtube:tab] Raise error on silent tab redirect
body:
Rather than allowing a missing channel tab to silently redirect to the Home tab, raise an error instead. This should result in less surprising behavior.

## Please follow the guide below

- You will be asked some questions, please read them **carefully** and answer honestly
- Put an `x` into all the boxes [ ] relevant to your *pull request* (like that [x])
- Use *Preview* tab to see how your *pull request* will actually look like

---

### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [x] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
- [x] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
- [x] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [x] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

See one use case in https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/2306 . There is existing code that special cases /featured and /live tabs which either redirects or raises an error. The code suggests that it was meant to warn on all missing tabs, however only /videos would be checked.

This PR generalizes the tab name check and raises an error if the tab does not exist (or is hidden to the user due to a lack of access). Any tab not specially handled and which does not exist will trigger an error. All changes are grouped under the existing compat option "no-youtube-channel-redirect", so those who set this option will see no difference in behavior.

The changes do not include a check for whether certain video views such as "Upcoming live streams" exist; such changes would be less trivial to implement. The current behavior for these URLs haven't changed, you will still be "surprised" if no such streams exist.

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Improvement
- [x] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

This patch adds support for ERTFLIX videos and series (optionally filtered by season) and embedded video extraction from ert.gr webtv. ERT (Hellenic Broadcasting Corporation) is the public broadcaster in Greece.

* Add new IEs:
  * ERTFlixBase: Base IE class
  * ERTFlixCodename: Generic video by codename extractor, it matches virtual `ertflix:<codename> URLs
  * ERTFlix: Extract episodes and series (optionally filtered by season or episode group), it resolves "tiles" and defers to ERTFlixCodename
  * ERTWebtvEmbed: Extract iframe-embeddable ert.gr webtv videos
    * Resolve stream and thumbnail URLs from templates hard-coded in the player page
    * Player page provides almost no metadata, so let the generic extractor override title

Pull https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/pull/30528 with changes.

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

This pull request fixes two things:
* Fixes full name extraction for all stories (seems to be no longer available in the story JSON, if it ever was)
* Fixes username extraction for story highlights, where the username is `highlights` and the story JSON only has the user ID.

These require an additional request to the Instagram API with a proper Instagram app user agent. The additional request is only made if either of the fields are missing or the username is `highlights`, just in case the normal JSON still has the proper info in some cases.

TED has restructured their site and a rewrite for TED extractor is required (#2343).

changes:
- rewrite talks and playlist handling
- added series handling
- removed handling /watch/ urls (was very old code and new watch pages no longer contains a single video as the old extractor expects)

I have used quite a bit of the old parsing code as some of them still matches the new json structure. There are parts of code which I am not sure if they are still necessary with their new player (e.g. audio_download and rtmp source), but since they are not fatal I chose to leave them as is.

title: [TubeTuGraz] Add new extractor
body:
### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [X] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
- [X] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
  - This is a port of [Youtube-DL#29160](https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/pull/29160) to yt-dlp
  - [Youtube-DL#26778](https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/pull/26778) is similar, but with less features. I have talked to @ruru4143, and he has suggested creating a new PR for this and closing the old one
- [X] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [X] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
  - I have written the bulk of the extractor code from scratch
- [X] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)
  - Some of the code is inspired by @ruru4143's PR, who released it under the Unlicense according to that post. 

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Improvement
- [X] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

Add support for extracting videos from [tube.tugraz.at](https://tube.tugraz.at), the lecture video site from Graz University of Technology.

Features:

- extract videos
- extract playlists
- extract restricted videos and playlists if login details are provided
- attempt fallback formats if metadata cannot be downloaded
- formats are assigned human-readable `format_id`s
- "presentation" formats are preferred over "presenter" formats

Reading the original PR ([Youtube-DL#29160](https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/pull/29160)) might be useful, since the comments to that PR contain some discussion about the detailed design.

The original PR got stalled because of coding style issues, although the maintainer of Youtube-DL refused to elaborate on what exactly was the issue. I tried to follow the coding style guidelines as closely as possible, and I can't really see an issue.

I skimmed the YT-DLP coding style guidelines as well, and it appears nothing has changed compared to Youtube-DL's. If I misread that, and the coding style did change, please mention it so I can fix it.

title: [core] Add brotli content encoding support
body:
## Please follow the guide below

- You will be asked some questions, please read them **carefully** and answer honestly
- Put an `x` into all the boxes [ ] relevant to your *pull request* (like that [x])
- Use *Preview* tab to see how your *pull request* will actually look like

---

### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [X] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
- [X] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
- [X] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [X] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [ ] Bug fix
- [X] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

Adds support for [brotli](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brotli) content encoding alongside the existing gzip and deflate (`Content-Encoding: gzip, deflate, br`). This requires either the brotli or brotlicffi module to enable.

Used by most browsers today, and sites such as YouTube seem to prefer to use it if available.

I'm not sure if this would make much difference to yt-dlp, but it seems simple to add. Open for discussion.

I haven't added it to dependencies in setup.py or requirements.txt since it may break some existing automated builds.
brotli is preferred if you are using CPython, brotlicffi elsewhere <sup>[ref](https://pypi.org/project/brotlicffi/)</sup>

entries if we want to add them:
[requirements.txt](https://github.com/coletdjnz/yt-dlp-dev/blob/yt-dlp-go-brrr-prototype/requirements.txt#L5-L6), [setup.py](https://github.com/coletdjnz/yt-dlp-dev/blob/yt-dlp-go-brrr-prototype/setup.py#L26-L29)

title: [Generic] Linux -- Fixed issue with cookies from browser with unsupported desktop session
body:
## Please follow the guide below

- You will be asked some questions, please read them **carefully** and answer honestly
- Put an `x` into all the boxes [ ] relevant to your *pull request* (like that [x])
- Use *Preview* tab to see how your *pull request* will actually look like

---

### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [X] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
- [X] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
- [X] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [X] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [X] Bug fix
- [ ] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

There was a somewhat recent issue with Linux where everything using cookies from browser would fail due to an AttributeError when trying to find the keyring. It was addressed in release 2022.01.21 (for the life of me I can't find the original issue). 

The new release fixes that issue, but sort of just moved it up the chain. When attempting to use cookies from browser with the latest release I get this: 

```
 File "/usr/lib/python3.10/site-packages/yt_dlp/cookies.py", line 333, in __init__
    password = _get_linux_keyring_password(browser_keyring_name, keyring, logger)

  File "/usr/lib/python3.10/site-packages/yt_dlp/cookies.py", line 793, in _get_linux_keyring_password
    keyring = _LinuxKeyring[keyring] if keyring else _choose_linux_keyring(logger)

  File "/usr/lib/python3.10/site-packages/yt_dlp/cookies.py", line 682, in _choose_linux_keyring
    logger.debug('detected desktop environment: {}'.format(desktop_environment.name))
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'name'
```

I just grabbed the last little bit of the log error. This PR addresses this issue by returning `_LinuxDesktopEnvironment.OTHER` if the `DESKTOP_SESSION` variable isn't KDE, XFCE, or Gnome.

- [X] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
- [X] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
- [X] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [X] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [ ] Bug fix
- [X] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [X] New feature

---

### Description

Add real-time progress tracking to ffmpeg operations, both for downloading (`FFmpegFD`) and post-processing (`FFmpegPostProcessor`).

#### How it works

A new `FFmpegProgressTracker` class manages ffmpeg subprocess I/O using a **Queue + Thread** pattern:

1. ffmpeg is launched with `-progress pipe:1`, which outputs machine-readable progress data to stdout
2. Two daemon threads read stdout/stderr line-by-line into thread-safe queues
3. The main thread polls at 50ms intervals, draining the queues and parsing progress blocks
4. Parsed progress (ETA, speed, bytes outputted) is reported through the existing progress hook chain

#### Changes

**`yt_dlp/postprocessor/ffmpeg.py`**
- New `FFmpegProgressTracker` class (~250 lines) handling subprocess lifecycle, progress parsing, ETA/bitrate/filesize computation
- `real_run_ffmpeg` now creates a tracker and passes `-progress pipe:1` when `information` is provided
- Progress hooks translate ffmpeg-internal status (`ffmpeg_running` → `processing`, `outputted` → `processed_bytes`)

**`yt_dlp/downloader/external.py`**
- `FFmpegFD` now uses `FFmpegProgressTracker` instead of raw `Popen`
- Always appends `-progress pipe:1` since `info_dict` is always available in the downloader context
- Progress hooks translate status (`ffmpeg_running` → `downloading`, `outputted` → `downloaded_bytes`)

**`yt_dlp/postprocessor/common.py`**
- `report_progress` handles the `processing` status with percent, speed, ETA display
- Gracefully handles minimal status dicts from `PostProcessorMetaClass.run_wrapper`

**`test/test_ffmpeg_progress.py`** — 82 unit tests covering:
- Progress regex pattern matching (single/multi stream, audio-only, N/A values)
- Time string parsing (HMS, ms/us units, fractional seconds)
- Duration computation with seek args (`-ss`, `-to`, `-t`, `-sseof`, `=` form)
- Bitrate parsing, ETA computation, filesize estimation
- Queue draining, stream accumulation, progress reporting

Closes https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/1720, https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/2826, Closes #1317
Replaces https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/pull/1133, https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/pull/1947

title: [crunchyroll:beta] Add self-contained support for crunchyroll beta for logged in users
body:
### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [x] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
- [x] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
- [x] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [x] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

The existing crunchyroll beta support simply parses the page enough to find the non-beta url, then hands off to the non-beta extractor. This no longer functions for premium content for US users, since the non-beta site forcibly redirects to the beta site. See #1911.

This implements direct support for crunchyroll beta through the API, but only when a login cookie is present. Otherwise, it hands off to the non-beta extractor as usual (this is in line with what happens with an actual browser as well).

Notes:
- This is literally the first python code I have ever written. It's probably pretty horrible. I've tested that it works, but the way encoding issues are handled in python isn't familiar to me... I pretty much just tried random things until it started behaving as I wanted in that regard, so there might be problems hidden away there.
- Probably could do with some better code formatting.
- I don't know that my format generation behaves well in sufficiently many circumstances, though it behaves similarly to the non-beta crunchyroll extractor in my testing. It's mostly monkey-see-monkey-do from other extractors, rather than well-thought-out code.
- It's rather unfortunate that there's nowhere to put crunchyroll's `sequence_number` metadata in yt-dlp. This would be awfully handy to have for reasonable filename generation, as some series on the site have non-integral episode numbers. See https://beta.crunchyroll.com/series/GEXH3W8G1/the-faraway-paladin for an example.
- No series extractor at the moment, but the authorization structure is no different, so it's just a matter of interpreting the api's json output for `api_domain + '/cms/v2' + bucket + '/series/' + series_id` with the same query string.
- There are quite a few different stream types I'm not looking at here. It's possible that a different group than `adaptive_hls` is the way to go.

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

Closes #1424 

Hardcodes some data that could be retrieved from self._LOGIN_URL if cloudflare wasn't so trigger-happy.

The data does appear to be constant, but I've left the retrieval method in comments so that if they do change, breakages can be easily fixed by anyone who can use a web browser's "view source" feature and knows a little python.

If anyone knows a way to reliably get around this and download the page, that would of course be preferable.

I have not tested this with the non-beta site, as I'm in the US and thus forced to the beta site, but it *should* work, and since it wasn't working before, it is, at worst, a no-op for those users.

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

This should deal with issue #2519 , including the improvements I proposed. I have tested the changes in a couple of channels containing the problematic video IDs, plus a few livestreams, and everything seems to work as intended. Regarding the video IDs getting truncated in the download log, I must have misremembered it because I could not reproduce anything like that. The download archive seems to work normally.

* Fixed _VALID_URL regex (now it looks explicitly for an optional third digit block in the video ID)
* Added '__plataform=web' to cloudac queries. Now referers and access token cookies are probably no longer needed, but I do not dare remove the code yet. _Just in case_.
* Added extra metadata: timestamp, duration and thumbnails for VODs, and timestamp (technically stream start time) for live streams.
* Added graceful(-ish) failure if a video or channel is not found or if access is restricted, using raise_no_formats. It now returns a brief error message and the error code found in the json, and hides the exception details unless -v is used.

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

This PR fixes the following bugs:

- On some Tumblr posts, extraction fails with a 403 Forbidden when trying to download the video iframe. (This was reported as [youtube-dl issue 29585](https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/issues/29585).)
- Vimeo video is not detected, since it is wrapped in an iframe rather than embedded directly in the page.

It also updates the test cases.

(N.B.: the embedded Instagram video's duration is marked as `0.0`. This is obviously incorrect, but it is actually what Instagram reports. Unauthenticated requests from yt-dlp get the `sharedData` structure and the bad duration; authenticated requests from yt-dlp, and unauthenticated requests from everything else I have tried, get [the `additionalData` structure](https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/pull/22880) and no duration. I have given up debugging this due to Instagram's absurdly aggressive rate-limiting.)

Thank you!

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

If you log in too many times in a short time period, Viki will start throwing a 429 Too Many Requests error and prevent logins to that account from any IP for a while (may be even around 30 minutes).

This will prevent that from happening by caching the session token. Viki doesn't seem to provide any info on when it expires, so login is retried if the API throws an invalid token error.

title: [glomex] Simplify embed detection
body:
The regex was trying to be too smart. Especially the `<x(a|b|.+)+>` pattern to detect attributes is terrible since it matches every `x` tag. The inability to use `[^>]` for `script` makes it worse and results in huge intermediate matches. Breaking up the regex makes memory use negligible while also giving 50-300x perf on random pages (from my naive profiling)

I have tested `iframe` and `glomex-player`. But can't find any example of `script`/`div`

@zmousm

Fixes #2512

title: catch BlockingIOError and report why ID was skipped
body:
### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [x] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
- [x] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
- [x] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [x] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

With the addition of advisory locking in #1066 this change is necessary to gracefully download the next video when a yt-dlp process hits an advisory lock `BlockingIOError`. Without this patch, when two yt-dlp processes are launched with either the same playlist or different playlists that happen to have at least one same ID, if one of the yt-dlp processes encounters a `BlockingIOError` the process exits. With this patch, the process that would have exited instead continues on to the next video, and the first process (this is also the case without this patch) finishes it's current ID download and produces the expected merged output file.

Videos with fragments also work as expected.

Implementation note: It is important that the yt-dlp process that encountered the `BlockingIOError` move on to the next video, rather than attempt to download the "next unlocked" part of the ID (such as the audio track) because that situation results in a corner case where the first yt-dlp process also hits a `BlockingIOError` when it finishes the part it was downloading and attempts to download the (perhaps still advisory locked) audio track. This corner case results in both processes not producing a merged output file. This corner case does not exist with or without this patch.

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information
This adds a new format filter operater that allows to filter video formats by matching the string based format fields against a regex.

Example usage: -f `bestvideo*[vcodec~="(he|a)vc|[hx]26[45]"]+bestaudio/best`

Alternatively, single quotes can be used to delimit the regex.

This implements my feature request #2681.

body:

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

resolving #2523

I also added another subtitle track that notes when a screen share began or ended

a few issues I encountered in this extractor

the automatic password entering is no longer working, this is due to a new "defense" mechanism set by zoom
it include extra headers in the http request for the video page that are generated by a js file that is also generated based on a seed
example of the js code:
https://hdq-colman-ac.zoom.us/assets/zm_bundle.js?seed=AADx7N1-AQAAsr6kOeqMVkDZFrY_h7kUEdue172Pbg3WR8ywwtLBJedku_Fm&uQHR71Sqnk--z=q

I tried to crack it and got pretty far, but I want to close this feature first
we can bypass this issue by extracting cookies from the browser after entering the passcode ourselves, [I use an extention](https://github.com/rotemdan/ExportCookies) because the `--cookies-from-browser` didn't work for me for some reason

another issue is that the zoom recording provided as test is no longer usable, it now requires a captcha and form filling to enter
so I removed it, I do not have a public meeting to replace it with
it maybe be usable if the test uses browser cookies after one registers to the zoom call from their browser, but I don't know how the test framework operates

also, I noticed that if I do not state output format and download all formats, they have the same name and so only one of them is downloaded, is that the intended behavior?

body:
## Please follow the guide below

### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [x] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)

- [x] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [x] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

AfreecaTV livestreams may be protected by a password. This PR updates the extractor to support the existing `--video-password` param, and to raise an ExtractorError if no password is provided and the livestream metadata indicates that a password is required.

Due to the temporary nature of livestreams, I don't know if there's a way to meaningfully unit test this; I can at least confirm by manual testing that it:
- [x] successfully downloads when the correct password is provided,
- [x] provides a suitable error if a password is required and not provided, and
- [x] successfully downloads unprotected videos as before.

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Improvement
- [x] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

This adds an extractor for the videos hosted on the <womens.afl> website, for an Australian sports league. Videos appear to be downloadable from anywhere (they work from up here in Canada!)

Individual videos are downloadable already, they are standard Brightcove embeds, however the site provides several index pages with playlists, including recent match replays, highlights, and other sections, though unfortunately with minimal consistency in how they're laid out. Some are under a `videos` prefix, and others are right on the root.

For that reason, I've made the regex explicit about what pages it supports, though I am open to suggestions for a better approach.

Secondly, the tests are using playlists which are actively updated - there aren't really any which aren't updated frequently - is there a better way to write these such that they don't degrade over time?

---

1. `SponsorBlockPP` is demoted from preprocessor to an ordinary class `SponsorBlockHelper`.
2. `SponsorBlockHelper` is now called directly from `ModifyChaptersPP` to fetch SponsorBlock segments just in time, rather than before all the filters (fixes #2536).
3. Added a single-entry cache for SponsorBlock segments to avoid making multiple requests when downloading multiple formats of the same video.
4. Apparently Highlight now requires `poi` `actionType`: https://wiki.sponsor.ajay.app/w/Types.
5. Ignore entire video segments: https://wiki.sponsor.ajay.app/w/Types.

title: [fragment] Add exponential backoff support
body:
## Please follow the guide below

- You will be asked some questions, please read them **carefully** and answer honestly
- Put an `x` into all the boxes [ ] relevant to your *pull request* (like that [x])
- Use *Preview* tab to see how your *pull request* will actually look like

---

### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [x] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
- [x] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
- [x] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [x] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

When downloading a HLS video I noted that many fragments were missing. Looking at the log, these fragments were given transient 429s in order to suggest to the client that it should back off and try again after a few seconds. However, the current retries happen as fast as we can go, which means those fragments just end up skipped.

This patch adds support for exponential backoff when doing fragment retries, which increases the chance that the fragment will eventually be provided in such cases. This is disabled by default, and can be enabled with --fragment-retry-backoff.

Example delays in seconds are as follows with the default 10 retries:

    >>> for i in range(10): print("{:.2f}".format(0.25 * 1.5 ** i))
    0.25
    0.38
    0.56
    0.84
    1.27
    1.90
    2.85
    4.27
    6.41
    9.61

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [X] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [X] Bug fix
- [X] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [X] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

This overhaul changes the underlying network request handling in yt-dlp.

The main goal of this was to abstract away all the network code to no longer directly interact with urllib for HTTP requests. This was so we could easily add support for more modern, updated HTTP libraries such as urllib3 to bring persistent connections, while also keeping our no-hard-dependency requirement.

#### TODO: https://github.com/coletdjnz/yt-dlp-dev/projects/2


## Changes

The new HTTP networking interface:
- Our own Request/Response/Exception classes that we have full control over.
- Introduces RequestHandlers - these send supported HTTP requests and return HTTP responses
- Introduces the RequestDirector - this allows us to use multiple `RequestHandlers`
- This allows us to support multiple HTTP client libraries (each contained within a RequestHandler)

Other Improvements:
- Fix issues with per request proxies on redirects for urllib
- Cleaned up various legacy code
- Support for `ALL_PROXY` env proxy setting (and `all://` proxy setting to cover all protocols)
- Support for `socks5h` (with `socks5` being treated as `socks5h` in the ydl layer)
   - Closes https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/6325, https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/issues/22618, https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/pull/28093

## Known issues / regressions
- Expected: urllib (and requests) handlers check the proxy scheme to see check if it is supported. 
   - urllib **does not** support https:// proxies, so it will not be able to handle a request with one.
   - Previously, urllib would erroneously treat https:// proxies as http:// proxies. It is possible some users have wrongly supplied an https:// proxy, so their configurations will break. We never supported https:// proxies and the behavior was undefined, so we should not have to support compat for this.
- Expected: if a user has `ALL_PROXY` set for any other program, yt-dlp will now pick it up.
- External extractors/downloaders etc. using urlopen() catching urllib exceptions other than HTTPError. We only provide compat for HTTPError, the others not. We could add compat but I imagine it is unlikely there would be an issue.

## Future
- Updating external downloaders to use `ydl.proxies`, `clean_proxies` and `clean_headers`
- Requests support (https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/pull/3668)
    - https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/1890
    - https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/issues/14523
    - https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/issues/13734
- RH Preference System (will be needed when adding support for curl-impersonate)
- curl-impersonate handler
- Websockets handler
- Disable redirects for a request
- Expose more settings/configuration to user
- https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/1191
- https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/pull/3615
- https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/1817
--

This branch has been force updated. Old commit history can be found in this branch: https://github.com/coletdjnz/yt-dlp-dev/tree/network-overhaul-v2
author: coletdjnz

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

Adds support for urllib3. Experimental. This brings us:
- a somewhat modern http library
- persistent connections (try it out on a large youtube channel or a video with lots of comments)
- [needs testing] support for HTTPS proxies (potentially fixes #https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/1890)

This adds an optional dependency for `urllib3`. socks proxies are supported without `pysocks` since we already have our own socks proxy implementation.

Resolves https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/issues/14523, https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/issues/13734

## TODO:

Main TODO being:
- [ ] exceptions
- [ ] improve redirect handling
- [ ] Persistent connections between extractor sessions may be a problem. E.g. In my testing, YouTube would send the webpage/iframe in an infinite redirect loop (captcha) after a while. This only seemed to apply to the reused connection.

More todo: https://github.com/coletdjnz/yt-dlp-dev/projects/3

title: [tumblr] Fix extraction; fix login; get additional metadata
body:
### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [x] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
- [x] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
- [x] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [x] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

This PR fixes the following bugs:

- Video height is not reported correctly.
- Some videos are not extracted. (Newer Tumblr video posts may use a simple `<video>` element rather than the iframe the extractor currently expects.)
    - Incorrect video may be extracted instead. (If a post is `<video>`-based, but the extractor finds an iframe somewhere in the HTML—for example, in a 'related posts' section—it will target that instead, leading to surprising results.)
- Login is out of date and no longer works.

In addition, it adds support for the API powering Tumblr's 'dashboard view', which is both the only way to view some posts and a good source of metadata. (Relatively little can be consistently extracted from the page HTML due to the arbitrary structure of Tumblr themes.)

It also updates the test cases.

Among the metadata available from the API is the content of the reblog chain, where applicable; I considered supplying this as `comments` but have not implemented it.

Thank you!

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

This patches a bug that extractor could return negative `duration`, which cause the following error.

It now ignores negative `duration` from the extractors. Duration with `0` is still permitted as everything seems to expect 0 or more.

Fix for extractor-side will be done soon.

author: flashdagger

title: [extractor] Standardize `_login` function
body:
Edit: Renamed to `_perform_login`

`_login` method is now a part of the extractor API and removes most of the login boilerplate.

The common pattern:
```py
def _real_initialize(self):
    self._login()

def _login(self):
    username, password = self._get_login_info()
    if username is not None:
        return
    # login code goes here
```

becomes:
```py
def _login(self, username, password):
    # login code goes here
```

and the more complex version of it:

```py
def _real_initialize(self):
    # stuff before login
    self._login()
    # stuff after login
```

becomes:
```py
def _initialize_pre_login(self):
    # stuff before login

def _login(self, username, password):
    # login code goes here

def _real_initialize(self):
    # stuff after login
```

docs:

```
    To support username + password (or netrc) login, the extractor must define a
    _NETRC_MACHINE and re-define _login(username, password) and (optionally)
    _initialize_pre_login() methods. The login method will be called between
    _initialize_pre_login and _real_initialize if credentials are passed.
    In cases where it is necessary to have the login process as part of the
    extraction rather than initialization, _login can be left undefined.
```

title: [crunchyroll:playlist:beta] implement directly through beta api
body:
### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [x] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [x] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

Closes #2930.

Also allows the crunchyroll beta extractors to operate when the `display_id` is the empty string, as I've observed to happen occasionally when videos don't have all their metadata set yet.

It currently functions at bare minimum, but there are some issues I'd like some feedback on.

The crunchyroll beta api divides the world up into series, which are further subdivided into seasons, which contain a sequence of episodes. Unfortunately, however, seasons don't get their own web urls.

Somewhat unintuitively, different audio formats (subbed, $lang dubbed) are represented as different seasons. The api provides `is_subbed` and `is_dubbed` in the list of seasons associated to a series, but as far as I can tell it does NOT provide a way to tell what language a season is dubbed in directly from that list, other than regexp-matching the season name, which isn't a very palatable option.

The current implementation just queries all seasons associated with a series and throws all the episodes together into one big list, which makes a bunch of unnecessary requests, as it's not uncommon to have ~10 different language dubs available.

- ~~I would like to implement some kind of reasonably usable method to select only the seasons you want (since most people only want one audio-language version, rather than all of them) and ideally only the episodes you want, without making a rediculous number of unnecessary requests. What should that look like? Keep in mind that episodes are not always whole-numbered, so the item number mechanisms won't always work well.~~
- ~~I would like to save the access token (what `self._get_params` outputs) rather than regenerate it for each episode, as it requires multiple sequential requests each time, but I'm not familiar enough with yt-dlp's architecture to have a good idea how to do that.~~
- ~~I have not tested it with other locales. The urls for individual episodes may not be generated correctly in those cases.~~
- ~~I would like to have the non-beta extractor detect crunchyroll redirecting to the beta site and switch extractors, rather than plow ahead assuming the page it got was right.~~

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please make sure the description explains the purpose and effect of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much context and examples as possible.

# Pull Request Contents

Add support for the domain https://muchohentai.com/

Brief info:
- Site uses jw-player.
- Videos are protected by Referrer URL.
- Video url is obfuscated in inline-js and reassembled on client side.
- Video url points towards m3u8.
- m3u8 is VOD type.
- Although there seems to be an authorize endpoint, it is apparently not necessary to download the videos.
- Sporadically, the site presents direct download that is protected by recaptcha. This feature is not implemented in this extractor.

Adds a new extractor for `livestreamfails.com`

While the API and media server URLs are unlikely to change, I still wanted to parse them from the JS file just in case.  
It's more likely that the regexes for that file URL and the properties in it break, so I use the current values as a fallback.  
I feel like the warning asking the user to open an issue should still be printed, so I didn't pass them directly to `_search_regex` as a default value.

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

This fixes a bug in the timestamp parsing that caused it to parse it as `YYYY-DD-MM` as `YYYY-MM-DD`. This silently returns an incorrect result when the upload day is between the 1 and 12, and causes an error when the day is between 13 and 31.

While we're changing this code anyway, I also changed it to use `unified_timestamp`.

title: Do not O_TRUNC writeable files before obtaining an advisory lock
body:
## Please follow the guide below

- You will be asked some questions, please read them **carefully** and answer honestly
- Put an `x` into all the boxes [ ] relevant to your *pull request* (like that [x])
- Use *Preview* tab to see how your *pull request* will actually look like

---

### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [x] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
- [x] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
- [x] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [x] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

#1066 has a implementation error, io.open() may apply the O_TRUNC flag to the underlying openat() syscall, causing the truncation of a file before an advisory lock is obtained. In the case where some other process already holds an advisory lock, O_TRUNC violates it. In the case where that other process happens to be yt-dlp, this results in a corrupt file, because the file will be truncated as the other process (the one holding the advisory lock) is writing to it.

- There's really no URL for individual songs; hence it's returning all songs at once instead.
- All songs are returned as `mp3` ext/codec. But it's correct, despite its extension of their URL (and `Content-Type`!) This has been confirmed by actually downloading the files and passing them to ffprobe. Repeat, it's correct!

As per [extractor/common.py](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/a825ffbffa0bea322e3ccb44c6f8e01d8d9572fb/yt_dlp/extractor/common.py#L174-L182), a fragment can either have a relative url (`path`) or absolute url (`url`). This also fixes the FranceTV extractor which incorrectly uses `path` for the absolute url in fragments.

The fix is copied from the implementation in [DashSegmentsFD](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/a825ffbffa0bea322e3ccb44c6f8e01d8d9572fb/yt_dlp/downloader/dash.py#L71-L74).

title: Proposal: Augmented download (with working PoC)
body:
## Please follow the guide below

- You will be asked some questions, please read them **carefully** and answer honestly
- Put an `x` into all the boxes [ ] relevant to your *pull request* (like that [x])
- Use *Preview* tab to see how your *pull request* will actually look like

---

### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [x] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
- [x] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
- [x] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [x] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

This introduces concept of "augmented downloading", to allow running side-tasks as needed.

### Design notes
- Each tasks are called "augment"s. They run background tasks required by some websites. (e.g. heartbeating, data rewriting...)
- These "augment"s can be called by adding `augments` key to infodict of formats or root. They do not require instantiation at Extractor-side, much like PostProcessors.
- "Augment"s may modify infodict to make subsequent code to use them.

This is intended to replace bloated heartbeat code, and to add HTTP rewriting server in the future (ref. https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/pull/888#issuecomment-1065670214 wtf).
So it fully replaces `FC2LiveFD`.

#### Content of `augments` key

```python
{
  "augments": [{
    "key": "heartbeat",
    ... # parameters required by each augment
  }, ...],
}
```

`augments` is intentionally made a list to keep order of thing in control. You can even run 2 heartbeating with different interval at the same time.
Augmentations cannot be dynamically filtered for now.

The currently defined `key` for each augment is only `heartbeat`, but can be added and checked at `yt_dlp/downloader/augment.py`

The code is currently rough, since it's at proposal now. Please feel free to review and provide suggestions.
author: Lesmiscore

title: Add `--retry-sleep`
body:
Adds option `--retry-sleep [TYPE:]EXPR` to sleep b/w retries.

TYPE is one of `http` (default), `fragment`, `file_access` and EXPR is an equation with `n` representing the retry counter

Closes https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/pull/2852 (superseeds)
~~`--exponential-backoff X` => `--retry-sleep "fragment: min(X * (1.5**n), 60)"`~~
`--exponential-backoff X` => `--retry-sleep "fragment:exp=X:60:1.5"`

### TODO

- [ ] Documentation
- [ ] ~~The list of allowed math functions could be expanded~~
- [ ] ~~The option name, types and function names are not finalized~~
- [ ] Allow type=`extractor`. Would be better to do after https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/pull/1649

- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [ ] Bug fix
- [X] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

Makes the http downloader much more lenient on what it retries on, to survive any sort of brief network interruption etc. (given enough retries for such). 

We are thinking it is better to retry on more even though some may be unrecoverable, vs retrying on few, known and likely to be recoverable errors but missing many other cases.

Resolves https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3056, https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/2071, https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/3034, https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/2969 and prob many upstream

Might also help sorting exceptions in #2861 simpler.

title: Fix format decimal suffix for exponents greater than 8
body:
## Please follow the guide below

- You will be asked some questions, please read them **carefully** and answer honestly
- Put an `x` into all the boxes [ ] relevant to your *pull request* (like that [x])
- Use *Preview* tab to see how your *pull request* will actually look like

---

### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [x] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
- [x] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
- [x] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [x] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please make sure the description explains the purpose and effect of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much context and examples as possible.

Before `format_decimal_suffix` would fail with an exponent greater than 8. Now it should be able handle larger numbers. While file sizes this large may be unrealistic, we can get size info from an external source themselves so it's good to handle this case.

**Before:**
```python
>>> from yt_dlp.utils import format_bytes
>>> format_bytes(1024**9)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "C:\Users\user\Documents\GitHub\yt-dlp\yt_dlp\utils.py", line 2291, in format_bytes
    return format_decimal_suffix(bytes, '%.2f%sB', factor=1024) or 'N/A'
  File "C:\Users\user\Documents\GitHub\yt-dlp\yt_dlp\utils.py", line 2283, in format_decimal_suffix
    suffix = ['', *'kMGTPEZY'][exponent]
IndexError: list index out of range
```

**After:**
```python
>>> from yt_dlp.utils import format_bytes
>>> format_bytes(1024**9)
'1024.00YiB'
```

Accept-Encoding header should be set for each downloader to what it supports, whether that is for native or external. 

This should fix #3142 

notes/findings for reference:
- httpie downloader automatically sets Accept-Encoding; it uses urllib3 underneath I believe.
- wget only supports gzip encoding (so potential issue in youtube-dl)
- Aria2cFD already adds the option to add gzip&deflate headers

Not sure about Axel or ffmpeg

- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

On some feeds (channels and search atm), YouTube has replaced the duration overlay (which is where we were getting duration from) with a shorts badge :roll_eyes: 

This PR adds a fallback to extract the duration from the accessibility data. 
I've had to modify `yt_dlp.utils.parse_duration` slightly to handle the duration format.

This data looks like this for reference:
```
WEIRD Sandwich Hack 😳 #shorts by Lionfield 13 hours ago 15 seconds 207,566 views - play Short
Italians get mad at VIRAL Tiktok videos by Lionfield 6 days ago 4 minutes, 39 seconds 168,049 views
this is it guys i quit by Yoe Mase Streamed 7 days ago 54 minutes 578 views
AN UNFILTERED STREAM OF CONTENT (Live Stream VOD) by Yoe Mase Streamed 11 months ago 2 hours, 24 minutes 678 views
# live
NASA Science Live: We Just Opened a 50-year-old Moon Sample by NASA
# in playlist
Why Self-Driving Cars DON'T Just CRASH by Techquickie 2 years ago 6 minutes, 15 seconds
```
(let me know if there are any edge cases I have missed!)


Related: https://github.com/TeamNewPipe/NewPipe/issues/8034 (thanks @TiA4f8R for alerting us)

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

This is an extractor for [ITProTV](https://www.itpro.tv/). It requires authentication via cookies (due to reCAPTCHA) and most courses are behind a subscription. This extractor supports individual videos (episodes) and playlists (courses.)

The tests for the individual videos pass. Both tests for the playlists fail but the extractor is functional.

For test_ITProTVCourse, it fails with the error `yt_dlp.utils.DownloadError: ERROR: "url" field is missing or empty - skipping format, there is an error in extractor; please report this issue on  https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp , filling out the appropriate issue template. Confirm you are on the latest version using  yt-dlp -U` likely becuse one of the videos in the playlist does not have a url for the format represented at line 115. When downloading the playlist outside the context of the test, there is the warning `WARNING: "url" field is missing or empty - skipping format, there is an error in extractor`.

For test_ITProTVCourse_1, it fails with the error
```
AssertionError: 'beyond-tech' != 'time-management'
- beyond-tech
+ time-management
 : Invalid value for field id, expected 'beyond-tech', got 'time-management'
```
as on the site when you navigate to a course url it redirects (30x) you to the first episode in the course. In the case of the test, the url redirect to is https://app.itpro.tv/course/beyond-tech/time-management

- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

The `BiliIntlIE` extractor is currently written to download content with a series and episode ID. I believe this is generally used for series that have been licensed from a studio (sometimes described as "OGV" in this market). This is similar to the operating model of, say, Netflix or Crunchyroll.

However, BiliBili also supports user-generated content (UGC). These videos have a slightly different URL structure, and pass slightly different API parameters (`aid` instead of `ep_id`/`episode_id`). This PR updates the extractor to support UGC videos, including:
* a regex update
* two `match_only` test cases
* a tweak to the data extraction logic to match `__INITIAL_STATE__` rather than `__INITIAL_DATA__` on UGC video pages
* updates to the API calls to support passing either `aid` or `ep_id`

I've manually tested that both OGV and UGC videos can be successfully downloaded with these changes. Note for others who may test this code: there seems to be a **pre-existing** issue with the extractor that causes sporadic 403 errors, at least for some users. As a workaround, you can retry the download.

Previously in this series: #247, #515, #948, #2686.

The Go case was caught, again, on issue tracker: <https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/2764#issuecomment-1038542729>, <https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/2818#issuecomment-1046365267>. This PR is *not* expected to resolve the overall tickets, though.

The fix for viu is speculative, as the reporter in the upstream ticket (ytdl-org/youtube-dl#30779) used the direct stream URL instead of the player frontend URL. It is not certain if this will work, as I have no means to test it.

As a side note, the viu extractor contains a somewhat curious FIXME comment, which I did not address here, but I think is worth paying some attention to:

https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/4628a3aa751ac0b2161b216662f0e959eb9bd206/yt_dlp/extractor/viu.py#L79-L82

title: [YouTube] Add new age-gate bypass
body:
### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [x] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
- [x] [Searched](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/search?q=is%3Apr&type=Issues) the bugtracker for similar pull requests
- [x] Checked the code with [flake8](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/flake8)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [x] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description

In a research project (https://github.com/zerodytrash/YouTube-Internal-Clients) I hammered the Innertube API with 8k requests in order to find a new age-gate bypass client. Turns out the client `TVHTML5_SIMPLY_EMBEDDED_PLAYER` with version `2.0` has no age restrictions in place and it seems to have the same success rate as  `clientScreen:EMBED`.

Request:
```
POST /youtubei/v1/player?key=AIzaSyAO_FJ2SlqU8Q4STEHLGCilw_Y9_11qcW8 HTTP/2
Host: www.youtube.com

{
    "context": {
        "client": {
            "clientName": "TVHTML5_SIMPLY_EMBEDDED_PLAYER",
            "clientVersion": "2.0"
        },
        "thirdParty": {
            "embedUrl": "https://www.youtube.com/",
        }
    },
    "videoId": "dqRZDebPIGs",
    "racyCheckOk": true,
    "contentCheckOk": true
}
```

Not sure if it still makes sense to try the `clientScreen:EMBED` clients (are they completely broken?)
I added the new bypass beside the existing one.
author: zerodytrash

title: [general options] add --force-extractor
body:
### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [x] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)

### In order to be accepted and merged into yt-dlp each piece of code must be in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/). Check one of the following options:
- [x] I am the original author of this code and I am willing to release it under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/)
- [ ] I am not the original author of this code but it is in public domain or released under [Unlicense](http://unlicense.org/) (provide reliable evidence)

### What is the purpose of your *pull request*?
- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Improvement
- [ ] New extractor
- [ ] New feature

---

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

This commit adds support for a new command-line flag, --force-extractor,
that takes as an argument the name of an extractor. This extractor is
pased to extract_info as the ie_key, which causes the given extractor to
be used directly without having to run `__init__` on all supported
extractors.

This is important because each extractor typically needs to compile a
regex, and all of that regex compilation adds up. In a profiler, this
extractor initialization occupies 80% of startup time on my test
machine (the rest is module import and checking ffmpeg versions).

Passing --force-extractor therefore gives the user a faster startup when
a given invocation is known to always need a particular, named
extractor.

body:
---

### Before submitting a *pull request* make sure you have:
- [x] At least skimmed through [contributing guidelines](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#developer-instructions) including [yt-dlp coding conventions](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#yt-dlp-coding-conventions)
