I'm not sure if this is the 'right' way to solve the issue (maybe stripping leading/trailing whitespace from filenames in general would be better), but it seems to be the way taken in eb55bad5a . This bug affects Windows at the very least, but I assume other systems are affected as well.

I thought up-streaming this fix might help someone - for me, aria2c performs much better with fragment downloads than the native downloader.

aria2 version used: 1.36.0 (latest)

title: [extractor/AmazonStore] Fix JSON extraction
body:
**IMPORTANT**: PRs without the template will be CLOSED

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

</details>

<!--

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please **make sure the description explains the purpose and effect** of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much **context and examples** as possible

-->

ADD DESCRIPTION HERE

Fixes https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/5110

Seems like the `_html_search_regex` the extractor was using was unescaping an escaped `"`, causing the JSON parsing to fail. 

I've just moved it over to `search_regex` and the other tests seem to pass (though the playlist titles seem to have changed, I verified and they seem to be correct).

Only issue is I'm not sure why `_html_search_regex` was there to start with. Was the JSON data encoded or something at some point?

body:

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please **make sure the description explains the purpose and effect** of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much **context and examples** as possible

-->

ADD DESCRIPTION HERE

See: https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/5106#issuecomment-1264566402

Embed wasn't being picked up as url in script src is not quoted:
```html
<script src=https://cdn.jwplayer.com/players/R10NQdhY-LzurxrFK.js></script>
```
Not sure if this is non-standard or not, but the web browser seems to handle it so we probably should.

title: [extractor/24tv.ua] Add extractors
body:
**IMPORTANT**: PRs without the template will be CLOSED

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

</details>

<!--

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please **make sure the description explains the purpose and effect** of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much **context and examples** as possible

-->

ADD DESCRIPTION HERE

Fixes https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues/4287

Adds support for 24tv's custom video embed.

I also added support for extracting embeds from articles. Sometimes the iframe embeds are url encoded and stored within an html attribute, but would otherwise be able to be extracted via generic. To make this work I extract the url encoded HTML iframe, decode it, then return it back to generic via a data url. It is a bit hacky but I'm not sure what other way would be best to do this.

Sorry for the duplicate PR as I failed to rebase my feature branch onto upstream.  This included other people's commits in my branch which you can see in #5120 .

This feature adds support for the video streaming website https://of.tv/. Two extractors have been created for single videos and entire playlists. While the generic extractor can handle this website, it extracts irrelevant videos.

Fixes #5017

title: handle list in graph
body:
**IMPORTANT**: PRs without the template will be CLOSED

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

</details>

<!--

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please **make sure the description explains the purpose and effect** of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much **context and examples** as possible

-->

A member `e` of list `json_ld` coult be an another list. 

Current code doesn't cover this case and a line `video = e.get('video')` could raise an exception `AttributeError: 'list' object has no attribute 'get'`

This MR handles a list case without an exception

```
[generic] test-porsche-911-gt3-touring: Downloading webpage
[info] test-porsche-911-gt3-touring: Downloading 1 format(s): mp4
[download] Destination: Test： Porsche 911 GT3 Touring [test-porsche-911-gt3-touring].mp4
[download] 100% of  184.98MiB in 00:00:06 at 29.48MiB/s  

Process finished with exit code 0
```

Fixes #5143

title: [XVideos] Support profiles, searches, channels and favorites
body:
**IMPORTANT**: PRs without the template will be CLOSED

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

</details>

<!--

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please **make sure the description explains the purpose and effect** of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much **context and examples** as possible

-->

Imported from https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/pull/30774

The XVideos extractor supported single videos presented via various URL formats. The site also offers various playlist-like pages:

    actual playlists: /favorite/ID/SLUG
    related videos/playlist: video URL + #_related-...
    channel activity: /...channels/ID
    channel videos: channel URL + #_tabVideos
    channel favourites (playlist): channel URL + #_tabFavorites
    search results: ...?k=(ID).

This PR tries to support extracting from those pages. 
----------------------------------------
Added `/profiles` tab support by me.

title: Fix MP3 EmbedThumbnail Metadata
body:
### Description of your *pull request* and other information

</details>

<!--

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please **make sure the description explains the purpose and effect** of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much **context and examples** as possible

Removed quotation marks around the 'comment' tag which was causing it to not be set properly. If the thumbnail metadata is not properly set then in some cases it could cause issues with audio software (e.g. Spotify tends to bug around if not everything is set in a certain way).

title: [extractor/reddit] Add fallback format
body:
</details>

Some Reddit videos (hosted on v.redd.it) have a higher quality video format that was not being extracted. 

`fallback_url` was already a field in the `reddit_video` JSON object: the extractor was previously using the value to find the video ID, but was not extracting the URL as a format. For some videos the `fallback_url` format's resolution can be up to 1080p, which is better than any formats found in the DASH MPD. I assume this is only the case with videos where the original source was 1080 or better.

Note: even though it is a direct http URL, the `fallback_url` is a video-only mp4 format, and Reddit seems to treat it like a DASH variant: the URL basename is usually `DASH_1080.mp4` or `DASH_{bitrate}.mp4`

Fixes #5160

title: [utils] `traverse_obj`: return `[]` if last path branches without default
body:
**IMPORTANT**: PRs without the template will be CLOSED

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

</details>

It is common to see a pattern like `traverse_obj(obj, ...) or []` since the default is currently `None`.
This can cause developers to fall into the trap of doing `for item in traverse_obj(obj, ...)`.
This PR addresses this issue by defaulting to `[]` if the last path did not match and no default was provided.

author: tkgmomosheep

title: [utils] `traverse_obj`: Allow `re.Match` objects
body:
**IMPORTANT**: PRs without the template will be CLOSED

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

</details>

This PR implements traversal into `re.Match` objects and related tests.
These keys are supported:
- `...`: returns `match.groups()`
- `function`: gives groupno, value as parameters (group 0 is the whole match).
- `str`/`int`: returns `match.group(key)`. Supports `casesense`.

title: [extractor/generic] Separate embed extractor into own function
body:
@coletdjnz Check that this works well enough your requirement. Will merge after you have confirmed

* I have tried to make minimal changes. But there is still changes to some of the extraction. But these only ever add more metadata, so it should be fine. Besides, there are changes that would eventually be needed for #4517 anyway

* It's not viable to make `_extract_embeds` a `classmethod`. But we still need to access it from other extractors. One option would be to move it into `common.py`, but then any related functions will also have to be moved there. Instead, Ive opted to create the `_extract_generic_embeds` wrapper. After #4517, if this function ends up being simple enough, we could still move it into common, replacing the current wrapper

* ~~I added a check to prevent accidental recursion of `extract_from_webpage`. Idk if it will ever actually be useful though. More importantly, do you think there is any use-case where it's harmful?~~

title: Optimize string concat in mpd parser
body:
### Description of your *pull request* and other information

</details>

<!--

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please **make sure the description explains the purpose and effect** of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much **context and examples** as possible

-->

I'm in the process of building a dynamic analysis tool for python. I ran the tool on this repository and found an opportunity to improve the parsing of MPD files. Right now string concatenations to parse initialization templates are done using `+=`. However, according to [PEP-008](https://peps.python.org/pep-0008/#programming-recommendations) the proposed change is encouraged and faster on most interpreters other than CPython.

I ran the dynamic analysis by executing the tests, therefore the proposed changes should already be covered by the following test cases: [float_duration](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/test/test_InfoExtractor.py#L1050) and [subtitles](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/test/test_InfoExtractor.py#L1262)

Fixes -

title: Aeon.co Extractor
body:
**IMPORTANT**: PRs without the template will be CLOSED

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

</details>

<!--

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please **make sure the description explains the purpose and effect** of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much **context and examples** as possible

-->

This feature branch permits extractions from https://aeon.co without requiring that the referrer argument be given. It works by matching the first instance of the vimeo video id, then passing a vimeo player url to the VimeoIE extractor, as a referral. Two tests have been added to verify a pattern in the placement of the hosterId.

It has been tested with pytest and linted with flake8.

Fixes #1653

title: [extractor/twitter] Full onion support
body:
**IMPORTANT**: PRs without the template will be CLOSED

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

</details>

<!--

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please **make sure the description explains the purpose and effect** of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much **context and examples** as possible

-->

This feature allows onion routes to match with the Twitter Extractor. It now no longer falls back to the generic extractor. You can call this with the `--proxy` argument but be careful as the twitter onion routes have aggressive rate limiting.

Here is the new terminal print-out for extracting a twitter onion link.
```
sushimint@sushimint:~/Desktop/Workshop/yt-dlp/yt_dlp$ ./__main__.py https://twitter3e4tixl4xyajtrzo62zg5vztmjuricljdp2c5kshju4avyoid.onion/TwitterBlue/status/1484226494708662273
[twitter] 1484226494708662273: Downloading guest token
[twitter] 1484226494708662273: Downloading JSON metadata
[twitter] 1484226494708662273: Downloading m3u8 information
[info] 1484225773611352064: Downloading 1 format(s): http-2176
[download] Destination: Twitter Blue - gm!   You asked (a lot), so we made it. Now rolling out in Labs： NFT Profile Pictures on iOS [1484225773611352064].mp4
[download] 100% of    6.17MiB in 00:00:00 at 8.24MiB/s
```


Fixes #3053 and [yt-dl 30736](https://github.com/ytdl-org/youtube-dl/issues/30736)

title: [extractor/bbc] Full onion support
body:
**IMPORTANT**: PRs without the template will be CLOSED

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

</details>

<!--

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please **make sure the description explains the purpose and effect** of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much **context and examples** as possible

-->

Added support for extracting onion routes from the BBC. They have two routes (bbcweb and bbcnews) which makes the _VALID_URL quite ugly. 

Unlike [twitter onion routes](https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/pull/5208), these cannot implicitly download using the normal web link so you have to use a tor proxy. Append the `--proxy` argument when executing to force usage of the tor network.
```

sushimint@sushimint:~/Desktop/Workshop/yt-dlp/yt_dlp$ ./__main__.py https://www.bbcweb3hytmzhn5d532owbu6oqadra5z3ar726vq5kgwwn6aucdccrad.onion/sport/av/football/63195681 --proxy socks5://127.0.0.1:9050
[bbc] 63195681: Downloading webpage
[download] Downloading playlist: Arsenal 3-2 Liverpool: Mikel Arteta says win is a special moment
[bbc] Playlist Arsenal 3-2 Liverpool: Mikel Arteta says win is a special moment: Downloading 0 videos of 0
[download] Finished downloading playlist: Arsenal 3-2 Liverpool: Mikel Arteta says win is a special moment
```

Fixes #3053

title: [extr/generic:quoted-html] Extract embeds within commonly escaped HTML
body:
**IMPORTANT**: PRs without the template will be CLOSED

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

</details>

<!--

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please **make sure the description explains the purpose and effect** of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much **context and examples** as possible

-->

ADD DESCRIPTION HERE

It seems that on some sites the iframe etc. embeds may be within escaped html -  such as in an attribute or json payload. https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/pull/5176 added support so we can write extractors that extract, unquote and run the embed detection for such cases.

This extractor aims to add support for common cases. One I have found is the `data-html` attribute, which is both used by squarespace sites and 24tv.ua. I am not sure if this is related to any kind of web framework or is just a convention.

If anyone knows of any other common cases, we should consider adding them too.

title: [redgifs], handle /users/ links
body:
**IMPORTANT**: PRs without the template will be CLOSED

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

</details>

<!--

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please **make sure the description explains the purpose and effect** of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much **context and examples** as possible

-->
`yt-dlp` supports for redgifs.com next types of links:

https://www.redgifs.com/watch/*
https://www.redgifs.com/users/*
https://www.redgifs.com/browse?tags=*

Current implementation of function `_fetch_oauth_token` works only with first link, all other links are raise 404 error

Current `master` branch fails `test_RedGifsSearch` and `test_RedGifsUser` cases

```
    raise DownloadError(message, exc_info)
yt_dlp.utils.DownloadError: ERROR: Unable to download webpage: HTTP Error 404: Not Found (caused by <HTTPError 404: 'Not Found'>); please report this issue on  https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/issues?q= , filling out the appropriate issue template. Confirm you are on the latest version using  yt-dlp -U

----------------------------------------------------------------------
Ran 1 test in 1.074s

FAILED (errors=1)
```

New changes pass these tests
```
[info] minordependentwisent: Downloading 1 format(s): hd
[info] Writing video metadata as JSON to: test_RedGifsSearch_minordependentwisent.info.json
[info] Writing updated playlist metadata as JSON to: test_RedGifsSearch_tags=Lesbian.info.json
[download] Finished downloading playlist: Lesbian
.
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Ran 1 test in 2.708s

OK
```

```
[info] kosherstarchygallowaycow: Downloading 1 format(s): hd
[info] Writing video metadata as JSON to: test_RedGifsUser_kosherstarchygallowaycow.info.json
[info] Writing updated playlist metadata as JSON to: test_RedGifsUser_lamsinka89.info.json
[download] Finished downloading playlist: lamsinka89
.
----------------------------------------------------------------------
Ran 1 test in 2.470s

OK
```

Fixes #5202

<!--

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please **make sure the description explains the purpose and effect** of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much **context and examples** as possible

-->

The PR introduces the following changes:
- Provide GraphQL API for future usage (this + #4864).
    - GraphQL errors are handled gracefully, extracting the error from the returned data.
- Allow usage of GraphQL for `TwitterIE` which transforms the GraphQL result into a v1.1 result.
    - In this transformation, Tombstones are filtered and returned as an `ExtractorError` with explicit message.
- Allow the use of cookies for requests (Fixes #1605), using GraphQL.
    - This also enables extraction for NSFW tweets with multiple media.
- Fallback to the old token if the new token gives a 404 (Fixes #5233).
- Stabilize multiple media support for cards (e.g. in [this](https://twitter.com/primevideouk/status/1578401165338976258) Tweet).
- Add an extractor arg (`force_graphql`) to force the usage of GraphQL on unauthenticated api requests.

All tests working with the v1.1 API work with GraphQL as well. Exceptions are:
- 17: NSFW fallback is not implemented for GraphQL, so it will issue an error if login cookies are not passed.
- 18 and 19: While GraphQL returns mostly the same data there are instances it does not.
    For these the returned url entities do not match, resulting in the description lacking some `https://t.co` urls.

Massive thanks to @bashonly for helping with this PR.

title: Fix YouTube clip end_time not being set.
body:
**IMPORTANT**: PRs without the template will be CLOSED

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

</details>

<!--

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please **make sure the description explains the purpose and effect** of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much **context and examples** as possible

-->

Fixes 'section_end' param to YoutubeDL.py being ignored.
This fixes YouTube clip extraction. Currently for clips, we download the beginning of the clip to the end of the entire video. The YoutubeClipIE extractor gets the correct start and end times as seen here: https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/commit/471d0367c76e1413bb35e0be45765a277e469ee2

However, the `section_end` param that is passed to YoutubeDL.py is discarded here: https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/blob/master/yt_dlp/YoutubeDL.py#L2723

```py
offset, duration = info_dict.get('section_start') or 0, info_dict.get('duration') or float('inf')
end_time = offset + min(chapter.get('end_time', duration), duration)
if chapter or offset:
    new_info.update({
        'section_start': offset + chapter.get('start_time', 0),
        'section_end': end_time if end_time < offset + duration else None,
        'section_title': chapter.get('title'),
        'section_number': chapter.get('index'),
    })
```

For YouTube clips at least, `section_end` will never be less than `section_start` + `duration`, it will be equal, so it is always discarded.

This was broken in a change involving YouTube chapter markers: https://github.com/yt-dlp/yt-dlp/commit/fc2ba496fd09ca68c7e6eeb2c11e7000d08ff099

This change fixes YouTube clips, but I'm not sure how it affects chapters. @Lesmiscore can you confirm this change does not break chapters?

Fixes #5256

title: Add mytaratata support through generic downloader
body:
**IMPORTANT**: PRs without the template will be CLOSED

### Description of your *pull request* and other information

</details>

<!--

Explanation of your *pull request* in arbitrary form goes here. Please **make sure the description explains the purpose and effect** of your *pull request* and is worded well enough to be understood. Provide as much **context and examples** as possible

-->

Added support for getting video url from jwplayer when it's initialized  through div tag ([source page](https://mytaratata.com/taratata/253/kevin-michael-yael-naim-lean-on-me-2008) for example below)
```
<div class="jwplayer" id="jwplayer-14944" data-heading="Vous aimerez aussi"
    data-image="https://mytaratata.com/media/cache/player/content/image/00/00/00/24/2480.jpeg"
    data-tag="https://7cd77.v.fwmrm.net/ad/g/1?nw=511351&prof=511351:ftv_html5&csid=www.taratata.com&caid=www.taratata.com/videos&afid=13220550&sfid=6503258&resp=vast4&vdur=1&flag=+exvt+sltp-slcb&metr=1023;_fw_gdpr=1&_fw_gdpr_consent="
    data-related="https://mytaratata.com/taratata/253/kevin-michael-yael-naim-lean-on-me-2008/related.json"
    data-source="https://taratata.net/253/239-720x576.mp4">
```

Also added test which can be ran with command inside a dev branch
```
python test/test_download.py TestDownload.test_Generic_72
```

Fixes #2690
